title: erisma parvifolium var. tomentosum
intro: Erisma parvifolium var. tomentosum, also known as the hairy erisma, is a plant species that belongs to the family Malpighiaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In terms of herbal preparations, the hairy erisma can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
